assumptions: The intragroup dividend declaration gives rise to a recognised intragroup monetary receivable and payable.
The entities involved may have different functional currencies, so foreign exchange differences can arise in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the IFRS 9 hedge accounting model, including when intragroup items may qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting for monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IAS 21 accounting for exchange differences on monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances whose FX effects are not fully eliminated in consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context separately includes the hedge accounting model for a net investment in a foreign operation, under which exchange differences are recognised in other comprehensive income and reclassified on disposal.
